title: Agency Mortgage-Backed Securities
gist: This is a NY Fed operational landing page describing how the Desk buys/sells agency MBS for the SOMA portfolio, not a Liberty Street analytical post. The only timely signal is a 06/15/2026 statement on an agency-MBS small-value exercise, consistent with operational testing rather than a new policy program.

insights.claim: The NY Fed Open Market Trading Desk buys and reinvests agency MBS for the SOMA portfolio under FOMC direction, executing all such trades with primary dealers and using dollar rolls and coupon swaps to facilitate settlement.
insights.speaker: NY Fed
insights.so_what: Defines the plumbing for any future QE/QT in MBS; primary dealers are the counterparties, so dealer balance-sheet capacity matters for execution.
insights.quote: Dollar roll and coupon swap transactions may also be executed as needed to help facilitate the settlement of outstanding MBS purchases.

insights.claim: The Fed first purchased agency MBS in early 2009 and has repeatedly been directed to purchase and reinvest agency MBS for SOMA since end-2008 to implement monetary policy.
insights.speaker: NY Fed
insights.so_what: Confirms agency MBS as a standing policy tool since the GFC; balance-sheet runoff or reinvestment decisions on this stock move mortgage spreads.

insights.claim: A NY Fed statement dated Jun 15, 2026 announced an agency MBS small-value exercise, the latest in a sequence (Mar 3, 2026; Nov 5, 2025), indicating routine operational-readiness testing rather than a balance-sheet policy shift.
insights.speaker: NY Fed
insights.so_what: Small-value exercises are housekeeping, not signal; do not misread them as renewed MBS buying.
